name: Filter Bar
description: Search input and filter controls for filtering page content. Sits above data-consuming patterns like data-table, card-grid, and activity-feed.

standard.description: Search input + dropdown filters + action buttons in a horizontal bar

slots.filters: One or more Select dropdowns for category/status filtering

compact.description: Search input only, filters in a collapsible popover

slots.filter-toggle: Button that opens a Popover with filter Select controls
slots.active-filters: Badge chips showing currently active filters

advanced.description: Multi-row with filter chips, saved filters, and clear-all

slots.search-row: Top row: Search Input + filter dropdowns + action Buttons
slots.chips-row: Second row: Badge filter chips showing active filters with remove buttons
slots.saved-filters: Optional row: saved filter presets as outline Buttons

slots.filters: One or more Select dropdowns for category/status filtering

visual_brief: Horizontal bar containing a search input with magnifying glass icon on the left, one or more dropdown Select filters in the middle (category, status, date range), and action buttons on the right (Clear All, Apply). Active filters may display as small removable badge chips below the bar. The compact preset places the search input and filters in a single tight row. The advanced preset adds an expandable panel with additional filter fields (date pickers, range sliders, checkboxes).

responsive.mobile: Search input takes full width on its own row. Filter selects stack below or collapse into a 'Filters' button that opens a bottom sheet. Active filter chips wrap to multiple lines.
responsive.tablet: Search and primary filters fit in one row. Less common filters in a collapsible section.
responsive.desktop: All elements in a single horizontal row. Advanced filters expand inline below the bar.

composition.FilterBar: Row(d-control, full-width) > [SearchInput(d-control, icon: search) + FilterSelects > Select(d-control)[] + ActionButtons(d-interactive)]
composition.AdvancedFilters: Panel(d-surface, expandable) > [FilterRow > Select[] + SavedFilters > Button(variant: outline)[]]
